name: Golden Cake Layers
recipeIngredient: 2 1/4 plus 3 tablespoons sifted cake flour (measure after sifting)
1 cup plus 2 tablespoons (2 sticks plus 2 tablespoons) butter, at room temperature
4 large egg yolks, at room temperature
1 large whole egg, at room temperature
3/4 cup sour cream, at room temperature
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 350°F. Lightly grease two 8-inch round layer pans with vegetable oil or butter, or line them with parchment circles or inserts.
2. Sift together the flour, baking soda, baking powder, and salt in a small bowl.
3. Cream the butter, sugar, and vanilla in a medium mixing bowl using an electric mixer on medium-high speed until light and fluffy, about 2 minutes. Stop the mixer twice to scrape the bowl with a rubber spatula.
4. Add the yolks one at a time, beating for 5 seconds on medium-low speed after each addition. Scrape the bowl each time. Then add the whole egg and mix until blended, 10 seconds.
5. Add one-third of the dry ingredients to the butter mixture by stirring them in lightly with a rubber spatula so that the liquid is absorbed. Turn the mixer on low to blend partially, about 5 seconds. Scrape the bowl.
6. Add half of the sour cream and mix with several broad strokes of the rubber spatula. Then fold in one-third more dry ingredients by hand, followed by the remaining sour cream, then the rest of the dry ingredients. Turn the mixer on low and blend until the batter is velvety in texture, 10 seconds.
7. Divide the batter evenly between the prepared pans and place them on the center rack of the oven.
8. Bake until the layers are golden brown and spring back to touch, and a tester inserted in the center comes out dry, about 35 minutes.
9. Cool the layers in the pans on a rack before frosting.
